name: project-graveyard
tagline: Scans local repos for abandoned side projects, autopsies why each died from git history, and recommends one to resurrect.
verdict: Solid, well-scoped skill: read-only scanner, strong consent gates before touching any repo, and a detailed report/output contract. Two dings: it claims to run entirely locally and offline yet later instructs web searches during the resurrection 'world-check', and it never addresses what happens if the scanner script itself fails (missing python3, permission.

highlights.summary: Description/body claim 'Everything runs locally. No API, no network, nothing leaves the machine,' but the resurrection world-check instructs live web searches.
'Everything runs locally. No API, no network, nothing leaves the machine' directly contradicts the later 'Search whether ... the world shipped the idea' world-check step.
Empty-graveyard case is handled ('no card'), but script failures (missing python3, permission denied scanning) are never addressed.
